items.summary: The Trump administration has fired several U.S. attorneys appointed by federal judges. Roger Rogoff is the first to sue over being removed.

items.title: 2 married couples from Honduras were among 5 adults who drowned in an Ohio river
items.summary: Honduran authorities have confirmed that four of the five people who drowned in an Ohio river over the weekend are Honduran citizens

items.title: RFK Jr. says cyclosporiasis outbreak is ‘under control’ amid investigation into its origins
items.summary: Mexico said its own investigation hasn’t found evidence linking the outbreak to the Taylor Farms facility in central Mexico.

items.title: Smithsonian too focused on 'oppressors and the oppressed,' say House Republicans
items.summary: In a House DOGE subcommittee hearing, GOP representatives excoriated the director of the National Museum of American History, following a White House report earlier this month that also criticized her.

items.title: Bystander severely wounded outside White House says Secret Service failed him
items.summary: An active-duty soldier who was hit by a stray bullet outside the White House plans to sue the Secret Service over the severe injuries he suffered

items.title: Mexican man killed after ICE encounter in Florida was on vacation, officials say
items.summary: The Mexican man who was struck and killed by a tractor trailer while running from an encounter with immigration and other federal agents in Florida had been vacationing in the U.S. on a tourist visa

items.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy fires his military chief after protests and names a new one
items.summary: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy fired Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi as commander-in-chief of Ukraine's armed forces following days of protests demanding his removal. Zelenskyy appointed Mykhailo Drapatyi as the new army chief.

items.title: Wisconsin Democrats want to allow absentee voters to cast a new primary ballot
items.summary: The Wisconsin Democratic Party is urging the state elections commission to allow voters who already cast absentee ballots to vote again

items.title: What to watch for in Arizona tonight: From the Politics Desk
items.summary: In today’s edition, Ben Kamisar previews the key contests to keep an eye on as primary results roll in across Arizona. Plus, our White House team digs into a critical new phase of the Iran war for President Donald Trump.

items.title: US sends food for Cuban residents while still pressuring island's government
items.summary: A cargo plane packed with food and hygiene kits has left Miami for Cuba as part of the U.S. government’s humanitarian assistance efforts for the people of the island nation

items.title: Man tossed by Yellowstone bison says hitting the ground was the worst part
items.summary: A man who was thrown into the air by a bison in Yellowstone National Park says the animal didn't seem aggressive until it picked him as a target

items.title: Ukraine’s Zelenskyy fires his commander in chief after protests and major rift
items.summary: The firing caps a major crisis for Volodymyr Zelenskyy after Ukrainians took to the streets to call for Oleksandr Syrskyi’s resignation.

items.title: Trump administration says it's deferring $1B in Medicaid payments to California and Minnesota
items.summary: The Trump administration says it's deferring over $1 billion in Medicaid payments to Minnesota and California because of suspected fraud and noncompliance

items.title: Nicaragua's President Ortega says no more elections, extending his 20-year rule
items.summary: The announcement effectively canceled Nicaragua's next elections, originally scheduled for November 2027.

items.title: 6,600 noncitizens were registered to vote in N.J. because of a ‘software error,’ governor says
items.summary: Democratic Gov. Mikie Sherrill said a review found that around 400 noncitizens cast ballots in elections from 2023 to 2024. The DOJ said it’s investigating the matter.

items.title: Is that food really made with 100% avocado oil? Likely not
items.summary: A new study finds that consumers who are paying a premium for foods advertised as made with avocado oil are probably not getting what they think they're buying.

items.title: The best way to clean strawberries so that they’re safe to eat
items.summary: I spoke to experts to find the best way to clean strawberries to make them safe to eat and to help avoid illness. I also recommend products to help clean strawberries.

items.title: Child actor Kaylee Hottle killed in car crash
items.summary: The 18-year-old “Godzilla vs. Kong” star was killed when the car she was riding in veered off the road and hit a culvert just before 3 a.m. Tuesday.

items.title: Should you use a bath filter? Here’s what I learned while testing them
items.summary: We tested bath filters to learn what they remove from water, how easy they are to install, and whether they benefit hair and skin. Experts share who needs one.

items.title: You can get Messi’s Argentina jersey for 40% off (and other World Cup memorabilia on sale)
items.summary: Messi’s Argentina Away Jersey is 40% off. You can also get up to 50% FIFA World Cup jerseys, collectibles and more.

items.title: US military identifies soldier killed disarming a drone in Iraq as a 30-year-old from North Carolina
items.summary: The U.S. military has identified a soldier killed while disarming an Iranian drone in Iraq as Sgt. Michael Emmanuel Swinton from North Carolina

items.title: Appeals court rejects Biden bid to block release of ghostwriter interview recordings
items.summary: A federal appeals court has rejected a request by Joe Biden to block the release of audio recordings and transcripts of his interviews with a memoir ghostwriter before he was elected president

items.title: Appeals court rejects Biden’s bid to block release of recordings of ghostwriter interviews
items.summary: The panel’s 2-1 ruling late Monday found there is a “substantial” public interest in disclosing the material that Joe Biden wants to keep under wraps.

items.title: The best games of 2026 so far, picked by NPR's staff
items.summary: Titans like Resident Evil Requiem and Crimson Desert dominated in the first half of the gaming year, with early-access sequels like Slay the Spire 2 and Subnautica 2 not far behind.

items.title: India's youth-led Cockroach movement vows to continue protest after police crackdown
items.summary: The movement, which began as a satirical protest, has evolved into a nationwide challenge to Prime Minister Narendra Modi's government.

items.title: US Carries out 10th Night of Strikes After Service Members Killed
items.summary: United States Central Command released video of its 10th night of consecutive strikes flying deep into Iran, targeting command centers, launch sites and air defenses. President Donald Trump says Iran “will pay” anytime they kill an American servicemember. Meanwhile, the State Department issued a rare global travel warning to Americans abroad after Iran sent another barrage of missiles into Jordan. NBC’s Keir Simmons reports for TODAY.

items.title: Iran-backed Houthis announce blockade. And, Cyclospora source remains unknown
items.summary: Iran-backed Houthis say they will blockade a waterway on the Red Sea. And, the FDA continues to investigate Taylor Farms lettuce as a source of the Cyclospora outbreak despite a retracted test.

items.title: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth goes to the Hill with nearly $90B funding request
items.summary: Defense secretary Pete Hegseth will appear on the Hill on Tuesday. He's looking for a nearly $90 billion funding boost, as the Iran war once again flares.

items.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on Canadian goods
items.summary: President Donald Trump on Monday imposed 50% tariffs on most Canadian goods, declaring that Canada has unfairly discriminated against American autos, alcohol and dairy products.

items.title: The best economic ideas from around the world — Planet Money Summer School is back
items.summary: Our free economics course for your ears is on a quest for great economics ideas.

items.title: Spain’s supremacy, Messi and Mbappé’s race for greatness, and a freak injury: World Cup quiz
items.summary: President Donald Trump makes a call to FIFA, Scotland fans make an impression, and an upset results in a national holiday.

items.title: Powerball ticket sales begin Tuesday in the United Kingdom
items.summary: Powerball ticket sales in the United Kingdom will begin Tuesday, allowing players there to compete for the same massive jackpots as U.S. players

items.title: Father, son charged with kidnapping two U.S. Forest Service biologists at gunpoint
items.summary: Joseph and Phoenix Henrichsen were charged with kidnapping a federal employee. “I’ve taken two fed biologists hostage,” the father is accused of saying in a voicemail.

items.title: 2 more convictions thrown out in 2020 plot to kidnap Michigan governor
items.summary: An appeals court has set aside convictions for two men involved in a 2020 plot to kidnap Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer

items.title: Trump imposes 50% tariffs on dairy, alcohol, and cars from Canada
items.summary: The Trump administration imposed 50% tariffs on three categories of imports from Canada, dairy, alcohol, and cars, citing discriminatory actions. NBC News' Monica Alba reports on how Trump is also considering additional tariffs due to the Canadian wildfires.

items.title: Pamela Smart, serving life, seeks a new trial
items.summary: Lawyers for Pamela Smart, who's serving life for orchestrating her husband's 1990 murder, are hoping that a New Hampshire judge will allow her latest petition for a new trial

items.title: Alaska wants to seize pilot's plane over a 6-pack. Supreme Court will hear his case
items.summary: The Supreme Court has agreed to review an Alaska bush pilot’s claims that state authorities have tried to illegally seize his $95,000 airplane after a passenger brought a six-pack of beer on a flight bound for a dry village

items.title: Attempt to save a struggling swimmer leaves 5 dead in an Ohio river
items.summary: Authorities say five people have died on a river in Ohio after several of them went into the water to assist another person who was struggling to swim

items.title: Trial begins for mom who says postpartum issues led her to kill 3 children
items.summary: Jury selection has begun in the murder trial of a Massachusetts woman who is expected to argue that postpartum issues were the reason she killed her three children

items.title: Tate brothers fight extradition to UK over rape and sex trafficking charges
items.summary: A lawyer says influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate will fight extradition to the United Kingdom

items.title: Sen. Darline Graham says she’s running for a full 6-year term after Trump urged her to jump into the race
items.summary: President Donald Trump said he had given his “complete and total endorsement” to Graham, who was appointed to finish her late brother’s Senate term.

items.title: Ex-Olympian charged with damaging Reflecting Pool gets Sept. 28 trial date
items.summary: A judge in the nation’s capital has set a September trial date for a former Olympic canoe racer charged with deliberately damaging the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool

items.title: Oystercatchers, known for sharp bills and beady eyes, are making a comeback
items.summary: A shorebird known for its sharp, orange bill and beady eyes is making a comeback in North America thanks to a yearslong conservation effort

items.title: How to keep babies safe when temperatures soar
items.summary: Infants, toddlers and young children need outdoor activity to build strong bodies and minds, but they also require extra care in the heat

items.title: US military identifies 2 soldiers killed in Iranian attack on Jordan
items.summary: The U.S. military has identified two soldiers killed in action last week as part of the war with Iran

items.title: We want to hear how Medicaid work requirements are affecting you
items.summary: If you have lost your health coverage or are at risk of losing it, tell us your story.

items.title: 3 women's world records set at the annual Lumberjack World Championships in Wisconsin
items.summary: Three new women's world records have been set at the Lumberjack World Championships in Hayward, Wisconsin

items.title: 5 family members found dead after flash flood in a scenic Utah canyon
items.summary: Five members of a Utah family have died after a flash flood swept through canyons in southern Utah where they were on a day outing

items.title: Trump celebrates the World Cup as a US victory as he presents trophy to the winner, Spain
items.summary: President Donald Trump has presented the World Cup trophy to Spain, the winner of Sunday’s final against Argentina

items.title: U.S. military identifies soldiers killed in Iranian attack in Jordan
items.summary: The Pentagon identified the soldiers as 25-year-old Tyler James Feehan of Hawaii and 19-year-old Isabella Gonzales of Texas.

items.title: Justice Department activates untested court for 'alien terrorist' deportations
items.summary: The Justice Department has filed a first-ever petition to a secretive and dormant court created 30 years ago to weigh government requests to deport so-called “alien terrorists” from the United States

items.title: Andy Biggs and David Schweikert offer competing visions for Republican Party in Arizona primary
items.summary: Rep. Andy Biggs and Rep. David Schweikert are clashing in the Republican primary for Arizona governor

items.title: The Nordstrom Anniversary Sale is here — Save up to 40% on our favorite brands
items.summary: During Nordstrom’s Anniversary Sale you can shop deals on apparel, beauty, accessories and more.

items.title: GOP prepared to block vote to replace Feinstein on Senate Judiciary
items.summary: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer said on Tuesday that he hopes to replace Democratic Sen. Dianne Feinstein on the Senate Judiciary Committee with Sen. Ben Cardin of Maryland and aims to set up a floor vote on the issue this afternoon, which Republicans are expected to block.

items.title: Supreme Court seems sympathetic to postal worker who didn't work Sundays in dispute over religious accommodations
items.summary: The Supreme Court seemed to side with a former mail carrier, an evangelical Christian, who says the US Postal Service failed to accommodate his request to not work on Sundays.

items.title: Anti-abortion doctors urge Supreme Court to keep mifepristone restrictions in place
items.summary: A group of doctors opposed to abortion asked the Supreme Court Tuesday to restrict access to a key medication abortion drug while other legal challenges play out, as Wednesday night's deadline for the court to act approaches.

items.title: McCarthy makes plea for Republicans to back debt ceiling plan
items.summary: Speaker Kevin McCarthy made a plea to House Republicans during a closed-door meeting Tuesday morning to back his debt ceiling plan, telling them that although it doesn't have to include everything they want, it will help get him to the negotiating table with President Joe Biden.

items.title: Fulton County, Georgia, jail leadership resigns after inmate's death and accusations of unsanitary conditions
items.summary: Three officials at the Fulton County, Georgia, jail have stepped down amid an investigation into the death of an inmate whose family said was housed in a filthy, bug-infested cell that "was not fit for a diseased animal."

items.title: China's economy is off to a solid start, rising 4.5% in Q1 2023
items.summary: China's economy is off to a solid start in 2023 following its emergence from three years of strict pandemic restrictions.

items.title: He was free for 2 years. Now Crosley Green is back in prison for a crime he says he didn't commit
items.summary: A Florida man who served three decades behind bars for a murder he says he didn't commit returned to prison Monday after spending the past two years building a life outside prison walls.

items.title: Family of victim and survivors of Indianapolis FedEx mass shooting file lawsuit against gun magazine manufacturer and distributors
items.summary: The family of a victim and several survivors of a mass shooting at a FedEx facility in Indianapolis filed a lawsuit against companies involved in the manufacturing, marketing and sale of the high capacity magazine used by the gunman who killed 8 people and injured several others in 2021.

items.title: McDonald's is upgrading its burgers
items.summary: McDonald's, which has been focusing on upgrading its core items to boost sales, is rolling out a series of changes designed to improve its signature burgers.

items.title: Google-parent stock drops on fears it could lose search market share to AI-powered rivals
items.summary: Shares of Google-parent Alphabet fell more than 3% in early trading Monday after a report sparked concerns that its core search engine could lose market share to AI-powered rivals, including Microsoft's Bing.

items.title: Tennessee Air National Guardsman applied to be a hitman online, the FBI says. It was a spoof website and now he's facing charges
items.summary: A Tennessee Air National Guardsman is facing charges after applying to be a hitman on a spoof "rent-a-hitman" website, according to the Department of Justice.

items.title: Even when wives make as much as husbands, they still do more at home
items.summary: • Four out of the five US metro areas with the lowest unemployment are in Florida. Here's why • Opinion: The overlooked problem with raising the retirement age for Social Security
